   \lean{AlgebraicGeometry.ProjTwist.serreTwist}
   \uses{lem:serre_twist_transition_unit, lem:serre_twist_cocycle}
   \source{nitsure-hilbert-quot}
-  % STATUS. The definition compiles; the sole unformalized ingredient of its
-  % cone is the proof of lem:serre_twist_cocycle
-  % (AlgebraicGeometry.ProjTwist.twistTransition_cocycle, a named `sorry` in
-  % SerreTwist.lean), so declarations mentioning the twist are not yet
-  % axiom-clean.  The glued object does not consume the cocycle proof
-  % mathematically — it enters only through the effective-descent
-  % (restriction) isomorphisms.
+  % STATUS. Axiom-clean [propext, Classical.choice, Quot.sound]: the cocycle
+  % lem:serre_twist_cocycle (AlgebraicGeometry.ProjTwist.twistTransition_cocycle)
+  % is now proved via the base fraction identity overlapUnit_cocycle_transport
+  % (factoring the three transition units through the common triple-overlap map
+  % commonHom : V(i,j,k) -> D_+(X_i X_j X_k), then Proj.awayMap_awayToSection),
+  % so serreTwist and every declaration mentioning the twist are sorry-free.
   For \(m \ge 0\), the \emph{Serre twisting sheaf} \(\mathcal{O}(m)\) on
   \(\operatorname{Proj} \mathbb{Z}[X_i : i \in n]\) is the sheaf of modules
   glued from the trivial line bundles \(\mathcal{O}_{D_+(X_i)}\) on the
